content:
𝗕𝗮𝗻𝗸 𝗔𝗰𝗰𝗼𝘂𝗻𝘁 𝗙𝗿𝗲𝗲𝘇𝗲 𝗔𝗳𝘁𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝟮𝗣 𝗖𝗿𝘆𝗽𝘁𝗼𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗿𝗲𝗻𝗰𝘆 𝗧𝗿𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻
As part of my continued legal practice in cyber fraud and digital asset matters, I recently visited 𝗧-𝟮𝟬 𝗞𝗮𝗻𝗮𝘁𝗵𝘂𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗧𝗮𝗺𝗯𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗺 𝗖𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗲, 𝗣𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗸𝗮𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗮𝗶 𝗣𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗰𝗲 𝗗𝗶𝘀𝘁𝗿𝗶𝗰𝘁, 𝗧𝗮𝗺𝗶𝗹 𝗡𝗮𝗱𝘂 , to represent a client whose bank account had been frozen following a Peer-to-Peer (P2P) cryptocurrency transaction.

My client had sold cryptocurrency through a legitimate P2P platform and received payment directly into her bank account. A few days later, her bank account was frozen after the payment was linked to a cyber fraud complaint registered by the actual victim.
Although my client had no involvement in the underlying fraud and had entered into the transaction in good faith, her account was flagged as part of the money trail during the cybercrime investigation.

Legal Intervention
During my visit to T-20 Kanathur Police Station, I: Met with the Investigating Officer to understand the basis of the account freeze.
Examined the complaint details, transaction records, and money trail.
Submitted a detailed legal representation explaining that the client was a bona fide P2P crypto trader and not a participant in the alleged fraud.
Produced supporting documents, including bank statements, P2P transaction records, cryptocurrency exchange details, KYC documents, and identity proofs.
Requested that the investigating agency consider the evidence and initiate the process for unfreezing the client's bank account in accordance with applicable legal procedures.

Many genuine cryptocurrency traders are unknowingly affected when they receive funds originating from accounts later found to be connected with cyber fraud. Every account holder whose account is frozen is not necessarily an accused. Each case requires an independent legal examination based on the facts, evidence, and transaction trail.

Need Legal Assistance?
If your bank account has been frozen due to a P2P cryptocurrency transaction, cyber fraud complaint, chain transaction, or NCRP/CFCFRMS investigation, timely legal representation can significantly help protect your rights and pursue the appropriate legal remedy.

𝗔𝗱𝘃. 𝗦𝘂𝗻𝗶𝘁𝗮 𝗦𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗮
𝗖𝘆𝗯𝗲𝗿 𝗟𝗮𝘄 𝗘𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘁
𝗖𝗮𝗹𝗹/𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁𝘀𝗔𝗽𝗽: +𝟵𝟭 𝟳𝟬𝟭𝟭𝟵 𝟲𝟬𝟭𝟬𝟵
